summary.title: [Authentication] An unlocked tab never enforces the 45-day expiry
summary.one_line_description: Authentication expiry is checked only during page initialisation, so an already-open itinerary remains unlocked after its saved access has expired.

issue_description.description: `initialiseAuth` compares the stored expiry with `Date.now()` only once, when the document loads. The only recurring timer created by `initialiseApp` calls `updateTripState`; it never revalidates authentication. A tab kept open across the expiry boundary therefore continues exposing the itinerary indefinitely, contrary to the stated 45-day access period. Revalidate the expiry on a timer and when the document becomes visible, then return to the gate without duplicating application initialisation.
issue_description.expected_vs_actual: | Aspect | Expected | Actual | |--------|----------|--------| | **Expiry** | Access returns to the password gate as soon as the 45-day grant expires. | The itinerary remains unlocked until the tab is reloaded or manually locked. |

summary.title: [Authentication] Storage failures leave valid users permanently locked
summary.one_line_description: A localStorage exception aborts authentication without showing a recoverable error or allowing a valid password to open the current session.

issue_description.description: Every authentication storage read, removal and write is unguarded. Browsers can throw `SecurityError` when storage is blocked and `QuotaExceededError` when a write cannot be committed. A read failure stops `initialiseAuth` before the form listener is installed; a write failure occurs after a correct password is verified but before `unlockSite`, leaving the user on the gate with no message. Manual lock can fail for the same reason. Wrap storage access, preserve a current-tab fallback when persistence is unavailable, and surface a clear warning that 45-day persistence could not be saved.
issue_description.expected_vs_actual: | Aspect | Expected | Actual | |--------|----------|--------| | **Blocked storage** | A correct password opens the current session and explains that persistent access is unavailable. | The authentication flow rejects with an uncaught exception and remains locked. | | **Manual lock** | Lock always returns the page to the gate. | A storage removal exception prevents the reload and leaves the itinerary visible. |

summary.title: [PWA Install] The install prompt can be lost while the password gate is open
summary.one_line_description: The `beforeinstallprompt` listener is registered only after unlock, so an eligible returning browser can dispatch the one-shot event before the application is listening.

issue_description.description: `initialiseOffline` owns both the `beforeinstallprompt` listener and the install button state, but it is called only from `initialiseApp` after authentication succeeds. On a returning device that already has the service worker but has no valid auth grant, the browser can determine installability and fire `beforeinstallprompt` while the password gate is still open. The event is not replayed, so unlocking leaves the Install app control hidden for that navigation. Capture the event during top-level script initialisation, keep the control hidden while locked, and expose the saved prompt after unlock.
issue_description.expected_vs_actual: | Aspect | Expected | Actual | |--------|----------|--------| | **Install availability** | An eligible browser can install the itinerary after authentication. | The one-shot install prompt may fire before unlock and the Install app control remains hidden. |

summary.title: [Offline Navigation] An online 404 can replace the cached itinerary shell
summary.one_line_description: The service worker stores every navigation response as `index.html`, including unsuccessful responses from an invalid path.

issue_description.description: The navigation branch clones and caches the network response without checking `response.ok`. Visiting a missing URL within the service-worker scope on GitHub Pages returns an HTTP 404 response, which still fulfils `fetch` and is written over the known-good `./index.html` fallback. The next offline navigation then returns the cached 404 instead of the itinerary. Cache only successful same-origin itinerary responses and retain the last known-good shell when the network response is not usable.
issue_description.expected_vs_actual: | Aspect | Expected | Actual | |--------|----------|--------| | **Offline fallback** | The last successful itinerary remains the navigation fallback. | Any resolved navigation response, including a 404 page, replaces the fallback. |

summary.title: [Cache Isolation] Activation deletes caches belonging to other GitHub Pages applications
summary.one_line_description: The service worker deletes every cache on the shared `culpable.github.io` origin except its own cache name.

issue_description.description: Cache Storage is shared by origin, not by service-worker scope. During activation, the worker filters only on inequality with `jalena-japan-trip-v1` and deletes all other cache keys. Because GitHub Pages project sites share the `https://culpable.github.io` origin, installing or updating this itinerary can erase offline data created by unrelated project sites on that account. Restrict cleanup to cache names carrying an itinerary-specific prefix before comparing versions.
issue_description.code_example: ```javascript caches.keys().then((keys) => Promise.all( keys.filter((key) => key !== CACHE_NAME).map((key) => caches.delete(key)) )) ```
issue_description.expected_vs_actual: | Aspect | Expected | Actual | |--------|----------|--------| | **Cache cleanup** | Activation removes only obsolete Jalena itinerary caches. | Activation removes every differently named cache for the entire GitHub Pages origin. |

summary.title: [Offline Updates] Cache-first assets can remain stale across online deployments
summary.one_line_description: Previously cached manifests, icons and local media never revalidate unless the service-worker script itself changes.

bug_6.file_locations: - /Users/sacino/japan-trip/sw.js - cache declaration and fetch event listener, Lines 1-10 and 42-50 - /Users/sacino/japan-trip/manifest.webmanifest - install asset declarations, Lines 1-25

issue_description.description: Non-navigation requests use unconditional cache-first delivery. A normal deployment that updates `manifest.webmanifest`, an app icon, Pikachu media or the OG image at the same URL but does not change `sw.js` leaves existing clients on the old cached response indefinitely. Navigation updates remain network-first, so the new HTML can be paired with stale assets. Use a deliberate cache-version release process or a stale-while-revalidate strategy that refreshes same-origin assets in the background while retaining offline availability.
issue_description.expected_vs_actual: | Aspect | Expected | Actual | |--------|----------|--------| | **Online update** | Online clients receive deployed local asset updates while retaining an offline copy. | Once cached, same-URL assets are served forever unless the service worker changes or storage is cleared. |
